title: The LLM players' endgame: a swarm-simulation forecast
question: When the 2026 AI capital circle breaks, what happens to the LLM players - who breaks, who survives, when, and how does the subscription subsidy end - as forecast by a multi-agent 'predict-the-future' simulation engine (MiroFish, on CAMEL-AI's OASIS, run on gpt-5.5)?
finding: The simulation's base case: the circle holds ~18 months then snaps in 2028 H1, from a FINANCING REFUSAL, not a demand collapse (demand and price are 'amplifiers, not the primary trigger'). Phases: expansion to 2027 H1 -> financing tightens 2027 H2 -> break 2028 H1. Most-to-least exposed: leveraged neoclouds (CoreWeave-tier) break first on an asset/lease/debt duration mismatch, then build-to-order hosting, then sub-priced AI tooling without ROI, then the component chain (HBM/packaging/networking), then the labs (framed via subscription economics, NOT solvency - no single lab named as casualty; Anthropic shown re-pricing); least exposed are NVDA/hyperscalers/TSMC (multiple compression, not solvency) and - the contrarian call - the gray-market relay economy (survives the financial break: no GPU-collateral debt, losses are operational). The subscription subsidy ends by RE-PRICING (quota/concurrency/context limits, usage-based billing), not by demand dying. Sharpest mechanism the swarm added beyond study 27: a next-gen accelerator launch collapses old-GPU residual values -> lenders cut the collateral haircut -> lease cash no longer covers debt -> refinancing refused ('2026-financeable does not prove 2027-refinanceable'). Trust: this is a SIMULATION, not evidence - the engine was backtested on the 2001 telecom collapse with its answer hidden and scored a contamination-adjusted 12/16, ~65% of output merely echoed the seed, and engine + study 27 share a model family so agreement may be a shared prior. Weight the two divergences (relay survives; no lab named) and the novel mechanism over the echoes. End-to-end value chain (rebuilt as 9 flow-ordered layers validated against the internal 28-layer graph + the Obsidian master map of 223 companies/24 layers, materials -> foundry -> memory -> compute -> networking -> power -> neoclouds -> hyperscalers/labs -> software): financials, Bloomberg analyst consensus and earnings-call management views all converge on the swarm's break order - leveraged negative-FCF periphery breaks first (neoclouds CRWV 93% lev/-9bn FCF, NBIS; credit-channel hyperscaler ORCL 84% lev/-35bn FCF) while the cash-rich core survives (NVDA +116bn FCF, big-4 hyperscalers, foundry, software) - even as the consensus prices the STEEPEST forward growth onto the very names most likely to break (NBIS +553%, MU +197%, CRWV +147%), and management is uniformly capex-up/demand-outstrips-supply (CRWV $30-35bn capex doubling power to 1.7GW; MSFT $37.5bn/qtr; Alphabet $175-185bn). Named financials confirm the break-order before any 2028 trigger fires: the most-levered negative-FCF names (CRWV 93% liab/assets, -7.2B FCF, -40% from ATH; ORCL 88%, -0.9B FCF, -30%; DELL 102%) are already the most drawn-down while the fortress core (NVDA 24% leverage/75% GM/+5.3B FCF, AVGO +7.3B FCF, ANET 36% near its high) sits near highs - so the research-grade expression is a PAIR (own the cash-rich core, underweight the levered periphery into the 2027 H1 refinancing window), with CRWV/SMCI already largely priced in and ORCL the cleaner less-priced-in short leg; our own news corpus independently surfaces the break mechanism (GPU-depreciation-crisis, ~30% B200 rental drop, CoreWeave loss/Oracle financing concern). Market-implied check (terminal short interest + forward P/E) asks whether the forecast is already priced: PARTLY - consensus on valuation (NVDA ~20x is the cheapest multiple in the complex; MU/SMCI ~10x already price a cyclical/credibility discount; the compression risk sits on MRVL ~59x / AMD ~52x / ANET ~41x / VRT ~41x, not NVDA) but CONTRARIAN on positioning (short interest highest on the fortress core, mean 3.68%, lowest on the short-leg periphery, mean 0.87% - the underweight is not a crowded trade, cleanest for liquid ORCL/DELL); un-priced edge = ORCL (-30%, 0.70% short, 22.7x fwd), CRWV/SMCI mostly already in the price. Caveats: CRWV's low short interest understated by IPO float/lockups, high core SI may be hedging not directional, stock SI misses options, consensus growth estimates absent from the pull. Research, not advice.
